2606.11853.title: Task-Aware Structured Memory for Dynamic Multi-modal In-Context Learning
2606.11853.paper_summary: Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) depend on in-context learning (ICL) for rapid task adaptation, but their scalability is severely limited by finite context windows and the growing cost of key-value (KV) caches in long multi-modal sequences. Existing memory compression approaches typically rely on rigid token removal or sample-dependent importance estimation, which introduces bias, disrupts semantic structure, particularly for visual representations, and yields static memories that cannot adapt to new queries. We introduce TASM (Task-Aware Structured Memory), a training-free framework that addresses these limitations through task-aware, structure-preserving, and dynamically accessible memory construction. TASM employs task-vector guided compression to replace sample-specific signals with a task-level direction that captures shared relevance across demonstrations. To preserve the underlying manifold, it applies semantics-aware token merging via bipartite graph matching, aggregating tokens without destructive pruning. Finally, TASM structures memory into a hierarchy comprising a compact Core Memory and a Latent Bank, facilitating query-adaptive dynamic retrieval. Evaluations confirm TASM maintains high performance under heavy compression, effectively balancing efficiency with adaptability.
2606.11853.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）依賴於上下文學習（ICL）以快速適應任務，但其可擴展性受到有限上下文窗口和長多模態序列中鍵值（KV）快取日益增長的成本的嚴重限制。現有的記憶壓縮方法通常依賴於僵化的標記移除或樣本依賴的重要性估計，這引入了偏差，破壞了語義結構，特別是對於視覺表示，並產生無法適應新查詢的靜態記憶。我們引入了 TASM（任務感知結構記憶），這是一個無需訓練的框架，通過任務感知、結構保留和動態可訪問的記憶構建來解決這些限制。TASM 採用任務向量引導壓縮，將樣本特定信號替換為捕捉跨示範共享相關性的任務級方向。為了保留底層流形，它通過二分圖匹配應用語義感知的標記合併，聚合標記而不進行破壞性修剪。最後，TASM 將記憶結構化為一個層次，包含一個緊湊的核心記憶和一個潛在庫，促進查詢自適應的動態檢索。評估結果確認 TASM 在重度壓縮下保持高性能，有效平衡了效率與適應性。
2606.11853.author: Zhirui Chen et.al.
2606.11853.authors: Zhirui Chen, Ziwei Chen, Ling Shao
